Short CV

Working as an assistant professor in the department of Psychology (English), Elif Kurtuluş Anarat graduated from Boğaziçi University, Guidance and Psychological Counseling Department in 2011. In 2012, with Dr. Feyza Bayraktar and Prof. Dr. Fatoş Erkman, she conducted research for the Turkish adaptation study of the Yale Food Addiction Scale. In 2019, she completed her master's degree in Üsküdar University, Department of Clinical Psychology, with her thesis titled "Examination of The Mediating Role of Perceived Father Acceptance and Rejection in the Relationship of Eating Attitudes with Loneliness and Belongingness". She completed the Doğuş University Clinical Psychology Ph.D. Program in July 2022 with her dissertation titled "Examination of the Mediator Role of Uncertainty Intolerance and Impulsivity Levels in the Relationship between Eating Behavior and Personality Traits of University Students".
